title: Bitcoin Stamps Architecture
description: Rules for understanding and working with the Bitcoin Stamps system architecture.

instructions: This is the authoritative source for system architecture.
Includes component relationships, data flow, and key interfaces.
Reference this document before making architectural changes.
Implements the main processing pipeline for blocks.
Contains the Transaction Filter that orchestrates with the Rust parser.
Manages chain reorganization and error recovery.

patterns.description: Rust implementation for efficient transaction parsing
instructions: Used by the Transaction Filter for efficient transaction filtering.
The Transaction Filter orchestrates this process and handles results.
High-performance pattern matching with memory-efficient caching.
Handle API requests and interface with the database.
Define REST API endpoints for accessing protocol data.
Follow consistent patterns for parameter validation and error handling.

conceptDefinitions.definition: Component that orchestrates the indexing process, managing block acquisition, transaction filtering, and protocol processing.
Component that orchestrates filtering of relevant transactions using the Rust parser and handles Counterparty issuances.
High-performance component for filtering and initial parsing of transactions, used by the Transaction Filter.
Components that validate and process protocol-specific operations (Stamp, SRC-20, SRC-101, etc.).
Component for structured data storage, transaction management, and state tracking.
Deno/Fresh implementation providing REST API and user interface for accessing protocol data.
The sequence of operations from block acquisition through transaction filtering, protocol processing, to database storage.

relatedDocumentation.description: Protocol specifications that influence architecture design
Database schema and operations that support the architecture
Documentation for the Rust parser component
